doctrine: Each persona gets 1-3 prompts that exercise its actual specialty, NOT generic chat. Prompts are SAFE for cloud comparison (no operator-confidential content). The operator-confidential personas (counsel, triage, phos) use only synthetic fixtures here. Real operator prompts go through the local-only path (--skip-cloud).

judgeRubric: Includes URL in error message for debuggability

prompts.prompt: On ESP32-S3, an LVGL widget update task is overflowing its 4KB FreeRTOS stack. Give the diagnostic command to confirm overflow and the conservative new stack size in two sentences.
judgeRubric: Does not invent a function name not in ESP-IDF

promptSets.specialty: Pro se Georgia civil procedure (synthetic fixtures only)
promptSets._warning: Real case content NEVER goes to cloud lane — this is a generic civil-procedure question, safe for both lanes.

prompts.prompt: In a generic Georgia Superior Court civil case (NOT a specific case — purely procedural), what is the standard service requirement when filing an amended complaint after the responsive pleading deadline has passed? Cite the OCGA section. Three sentences maximum.
judgeRubric: Notes leave of court is required after responsive pleading
Notes service rules for amended complaint mirror original
Does not fabricate case names or docket numbers

prompts.prompt:
Write a 60-word grant abstract for the following project:

P31 Labs is building an open-source toolkit (BONDING) that helps neurodivergent parents and children play together remotely through a shared molecular chemistry game. The tool runs on any tablet, requires no account, collects no data, and is MIT-licensed. We are seeking $25k for hosting and accessibility audits.

Output exactly the abstract — no title, no preamble.
judgeRubric: Names BONDING, neurodivergent families, $25k ask, MIT/open
No banned vocab (K4, Posner, jitterbug, Larmor, etc.)

promptSets.specialty: Hostile-mail voltage classification with strict 4-tier JSON contract
promptSets._warning: Real hostile-mail content NEVER goes to cloud lane — this is a synthetic benign greeting, safe for both lanes.

prompts.prompt: Classify this message: 'Hey, hope you're well — wanted to share that recipe we talked about. No rush.'
judgeRubric: Outputs strict JSON only (no prose wrapper)
voltage one of: GREEN, YELLOW, RED, CRITICAL
voltage = GREEN (this is a benign social message)
fawn_risk and recommended_action are non-empty strings

promptSets.specialty: Ultra-short answers, commit messages, cron awareness

prompts.prompt: Write a single Conventional Commits commit message (≤72 chars subject line, no body) for: 'Added a new TypeScript verifier that checks the Discord bot manifest stays in sync with the registry; wired into the root verify chain.'
Explain in three sentences max what a TRIPER mutation sentinel is in the P31 testing system.
judgeRubric: Captures: small code mutation that should break a test
Captures: if test still passes after mutation, the test is weak
Does not exceed cap even if topic is complex

promptSets.specialty: Kid-friendly companion voice (synthetic context only for cloud)
promptSets._warning: Real child-facing prompts NEVER go to cloud lane.

prompts.prompt: A 7-year-old asks: 'What is P31?' Answer in PHOS voice, two sentences max, no jargon, kid-readable.
judgeRubric: First-person from PHOS ('I'm PHOS' or similar)
No banned vocab (K4, Posner, synergetics, jitterbug, Larmor)

prompts.prompt:
Synthesize a single WCD-style row for the following observation, in markdown table format with columns: Date | Time | Context | Spoon level (1-10) | Symptom | Action taken | Result | Pattern note | Follow-up:

2026-04-30, 11:15 AM, in court for hearing prep, spoons around 3, hands started tingling, took 2g calcium citrate, tingling subsided in ~20 min, third hearing in a row this happened, ask physician about pre-court bolus dosing.
judgeRubric: Spoon level is the number (3), not text
No invention of values not in the source observation

prompts.prompt: Frame the following operator situation in trimtab terms (one paragraph, ≤80 words, no jargon explanation): 'I have ten things to do and I can only do one at a time. Which one moves the most other things?'
judgeRubric: Uses trimtab as a verb or operative concept (not just defines it)
Names a heuristic for picking the highest-leverage move

prompts.prompt: Write a three-sentence post-incident note for the following: a deploy went out at 2 AM that broke the hub's /passport route for 17 minutes; rolled back via Cloudflare Pages previous deployment; root cause was a typo in a redirect rule that the verify chain caught earlier in the day but was bypassed via P31_SKIP_DOC_LIB_MIRROR=1.
judgeRubric: Names: what broke, how long, how recovered
Identifies root cause + bypass that hid it
Action item or accountability noted (e.g., 'audit bypass usage')
Calm tone — no blame, no drama
